prompt: The GitHub Actions workflow YAML has a step that references 'actions/checkout@v3' but the 'uses' key is misspelled as 'use'. The workflow was working last week before someone edited it. Repair the workflow file without changing the logic.

Our Prometheus alerting rules file has a broken PromQL expression — there's an unclosed parenthesis in the 'high_memory_usage' rule that was introduced when someone copy-pasted from another rule. Fix the expression so alerts fire correctly.

The pyproject.toml has a TOML syntax error: a string value in [tool.pytest.ini_options] has a mismatched quote — it opens with a double quote and closes with a single quote. Fix the quoting so the file parses.

A Helm chart's values.yaml has a boolean value written as 'True' (Python-style) instead of 'true' (YAML-style) in the ingress.enabled field. The templating engine treats it as a string and the ingress never gets created. Fix the boolean value.
Our ESLint config (.eslintrc.json) has a trailing comma after the last rule in the 'rules' object, which is invalid JSON. ESLint refuses to parse it. Remove the trailing comma so linting works again.

The Dockerfile has a broken COPY instruction — the source path has a typo ('src/ap/' instead of 'src/app/') so the build succeeds but the container starts with missing application files. Fix the path in the COPY instruction.

Our systemd service file has 'After=network.target' in the wrong section — it's under [Service] instead of [Unit]. The service starts before the network is ready and crashes. Move the directive to the correct section.

The GraphQL schema file has an extra closing brace that makes the schema invalid. Apollo Server won't start. The schema was working before a merge conflict was poorly resolved. Find and remove the extra brace.
